title: Reachability Over Format
reason: A clever agent-readiness finding from Liad/ORA: the format of agent files matters less than whether agents can actually discover and reach them from trusted surfaces.
expansionBrief: Keep this concept tied to Rebuilding the web for agents, ORA research, agent-readable docs/homepages, and practical site changes that make agent guidance reachable.

title: Nearly Headless Web
reason: A useful product/design frame from Liad's talk: the web becomes mostly machine-operable while preserving human-visible moments for judgment and handoff.
expansionBrief: Connect this to WebMCP, MCP Apps, human handoff, browser fallback, and cases where full headless automation is the wrong abstraction.
